verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If ye love Me, keep My commandments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And I will pray the Father, and He shall give you another Comforter, that He may abide with you forever; even the Spirit of truth, whom the world cannot receive, because it seeth Him not, neither knoweth Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We need everywhere works and actions, not a mere show of words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to say and to promise is easy for any one, but to act is not equally easy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why have I made these remarks?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because there are many at this time who say that they fear and love God, but in their works show the contrary; but God requireth that love which is shown by works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He said to the disciples, “If ye love Me, keep My commandments.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For after He had told them, “Whatsoever ye shall ask, I will do it,” that they might not deem the mere “asking” to be availing, He added, “If ye love Me,” “then,” He saith, “I will do it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And since it was likely that they would be troubled when they heard that, “I go to the Father,” He telleth them “to be troubled now is not to love, to love is to obey My words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I have given you a commandment that ye love one another, that ye do so to each other as I have done to you; this is love, to obey these My words, and to yield to Him who is the object of your love.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And I will ask the Father, and He shall give you another Comforter.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again His speech is one of condescension.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since it was probable, that they not yet knowing Him would eagerly seek His society, His discourse, His presence in the flesh, and would admit of no consolation when He was absent, what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I will ask the Father, and He shall give you another Comforter,” that is, “Another like unto Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let those be ashamed who have the disease of Sabellius, who hold not the fitting opinion concerning the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the marvel of this discourse is this, that it hath stricken down contradictory heresies with the same blow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For by saying “another,” He showeth the difference of Person, and by “Paraclete,” the connection of Substance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why said He, “I will ask the Father”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because had He said, “I will send Him,” they would not have so much believed and now the object is that He should be believed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For afterwards He declares that He Himself sendeth Him, saying, “Receive ye the Holy Ghost” ( c. xx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 22 ); but in this place He telleth them that He asketh the Father, so as to render His discourse credible to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since John saith of Him, “Of His fullness have all we received” ( c. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 16 ); but what He had, how receiveth He from another?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again, “He shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost and with fire.” ( Luke iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But what had He more than the Apostles, if He was about to ask It of His Father in order to give It to others, when they often even without prayer appear to have done thus?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And how, if It is sent according to request from the Father, doth It descend of Itself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And how is that which is everywhere present sent by Another, that which “divideth to every man severally as He will” ( 1 Cor. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 11 ), and which saith with authority, “Separate Me Paul and Barnabas”? ( Acts xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Those ministers were ministering unto God, yet still It called them authoritatively to Its own work; not that It called them to any different work, but in order to show Its power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What then,” saith some one, “is, ‘I will ask the Father’?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: (He saith it) to show the time of Its coming.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when He had cleansed them by the sacrifice, then the Holy Ghost lighted upon them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And why, while He was with them, came it not?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because the sacrifice was not yet offered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when afterwards sin had been loosed, and they were being sent forth to dangers, and were stripping themselves for the contest, then need was that the Anointer should come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But why did not the Spirit come immediately after the Resurrection?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In order that being greatly desirous of It, they might receive It with great joy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as long as Christ was with them, they were not in tribulation; but when He departed, being made defenseless and thrown into much fear, they would receive It with much readiness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He remaineth with you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This showeth that even after death It departeth not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But lest when they heard of the “Paraclete,” they should imagine a second Incarnation, and expect to see It with their eyes, He setteth them right by saying, “Whom the world cannot receive, because it seeth Him not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He will not be with you as I have been, but will dwell in your very souls”; for this is the, “shall be in you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He calleth it the “Spirit of truth”; thus explaining the types in the Old Testament.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That He may be with you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is, “may be with you”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That which He saith Himself, that “I am with you.” ( Matt. xxviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, He also implieth something else, that “the case of the Spirit shall not be the same as Mine, He shall never leave you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Whom the world cannot receive, because it seeth Him not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why, what is there belonging to the other Persons that is visible?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nothing; but He speaketh here of knowledge; at least He addeth, “neither knoweth Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He is wont, in the case of exact knowledge, to call it “sight”; because sight is clearer than the other senses, by this He always representeth exact knowledge.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By “world,” He here speaketh of “the wicked,” thus too comforting the disciples by giving to them a special gift.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: See in how many particulars He raised His discourse concerning It.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said, “He is Another like unto Me”; He said, “He will not leave you”; He said, “Unto you alone He cometh, as also did I”; He said, that “He remaineth in you”; but not even so did He drive out their despondency.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they still sought Him and His society.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To cure then this feeling, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I will not leave you orphans, I will come unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Fear not,” He saith, “I said not that I would send you another Comforter, as though I were Myself withdrawing from you for ever; I said not that He remaineth with you, as though I should see you no more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For I also Myself will come to you, I will not leave you orphans.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because when commencing He said, “Little children,” therefore He saith also here, “I will not leave you orphans.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At first then He told them, “Ye shall come whither I go”; and, “In My Father’s house there are many mansions”; but here, since that time was long, He giveth them the Spirit; and when, not knowing what it could be of which He spoke, they were not sufficiently comforted, “I will not leave you orphans,” He saith; for this they chiefly required.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But since the, “I will come to you,” was the saying of one declaring a “presence,” observe how in order that they might not again seek for the same kind of presence as before, He did not clearly tell them this thing, but hinted at it; for having said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Yet a little while, and the world seeth Me not”; He added, “but ye see Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As though He had said, “I come indeed to you, but not in the same way as before, ever being with you day by day.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And lest they should say, “How then saidst Thou to the Jews, Henceforth ye shall not see Me?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He solveth the contradiction by saying, “to you alone”; for such also is the nature of the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Because I live, ye shall live also.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the Cross doth not finally separate us, but only hideth for a little moment; and by “life” He seemeth to me to mean not the present only, but the future also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “At that day ye shall know that am in the Father, and you in Me, and I in you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: With regard to the Father, these words refer to Essence; with regard to the disciples, to agreement of mind and help from God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And how, tell me, is this reasonable?” saith some one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And how, pray, is the contrary reasonable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For great and altogether boundless is the interval between Christ and the disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if the same words are employed, marvel not; for the Scripture is often wont to use in different senses the same words, when applied to God and to men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus we are called “gods,” and “sons of God,” yet the word hath not the same force when applied to us and to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the Son is called “Image,” and “Glory”; so are we, but great is the interval between us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, “Ye are Christ’s, and Christ is God’s” ( 1 Cor. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 23 ), but not in like manner as Christ is God’s are we Christ’s.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what is it that He saith?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “When I am arisen,” He saith, “ye shall know that I am not separated from the Father, but have the same power with Him, and that I am with you continually, when facts proclaim the aid which cometh to you from Me, when your enemies are kept down, and you speak boldly, when dangers are removed from your path, when the preaching of the Gospel flourisheth day by day, when all yield and give ground to the word of true religion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “As the Father hath sent Me, so send I you.” ( c. xx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that here also the word hath not the same force? for if we take it as though it had, the Apostles will differ in nothing from Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why saith He, “Then ye shall know”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because then they saw Him risen and conversing with them, then they learnt the exact faith; for great was the power of the Spirit, which taught them all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that hath My commandments and keepeth them, he it is that loveth Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is not enough merely to have them, we need also an exact keeping of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why doth He frequently say the same thing to them? as, “If ye love Me, ye will keep My commandments” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 15 ); and, “He that hath My commandments and keepeth them”; and, “If any one heareth My word and keepeth it, he it is that loveth Me—he that heareth not My words, loveth Me not.” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I think that He alluded to their despondency; for since He had uttered many wise sayings to them concerning death, saying, “He that hateth his life in this world shall save it unto life eternal” ( c. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 25 ); and, “Unless a man take his cross and follow Me, he is not worthy of Me” ( Matt. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 38 ); and is about to say other things besides, rebuking them, He saith, “Think ye that ye suffer sorrow from love?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The not sorrowing would be a sign of love.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And because He wished all along to establish this, as He went on He summed up His discourse in this same point; “If ye loved Me,” He saith, “ye would have rejoiced, because—I go to My Father” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 28 ), but now ye are in this state through cowardice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To be thus disposed towards death is not for those who remember My commandments; for you ought to be crucified, if you truly loved Me, for My word exhorteth you not to be afraid of those that kill the body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Those that are such both the Father loveth and I.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And I will manifest Myself unto him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then saith Judas,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How is it that Thou wilt manifest Thyself unto us?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that their soul was close pressed with fear?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he was confounded and troubled, and thought that as we see dead men in a dream, so He also would be seen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In order therefore that they might not imagine this, hear what He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I and the Father will come unto him, and make Our abode with him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: All but saying, “As the Father revealeth Himself, so also do I.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And not in this way only He removed the suspicion, but also by saying, “We will make Our abode with him,” a thing which doth not belong to dreams.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But observe, I pray you, the disciple confounded, and not daring to say plainly what he desired to say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he said not, “Woe to us, that Thou diest, and will come to us as the dead come”; he spake not thus; but, “How is it that Thou wilt show Thyself to us, and not unto the world?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Jesus then saith, that “I accept you, because ye keep My commandments.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In order that they might not, when they should see Him afterwards, deem Him to be an apparition, therefore He saith these things beforehand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that they might not deem that He would appear to them so as I have said, He telleth them also the reason, “Because ye keep My commandments”; He saith that the Spirit also will appear in like manner.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if after having companied with Him so long time, they cannot yet endure that Essence, or rather cannot even imagine It, what would have been their case had He appeared thus to them at the first? on this account also He ate with them, that the action might not seem to be an illusion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if they thought this when they saw Him walking on the waters, although His wonted form was seen by them, and He was not far distant, what would they have imagined had they suddenly seen Him arisen whom they had seen taken and swathed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He continually telleth them that He will appear, and why He will appear, and how, that they may not suppose Him to be an apparition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that loveth Me not keepeth not My sayings; and the word which ye hear is not Mine, but the Father’s which sent Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “So that he that heareth not these sayings not only doth not love Me, but neither doth he love the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if this is the sure proof of love, the hearing the commandments, and these are of the Father, he that heareth them loveth not the Son only, but the Father also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And how is the word ‘thine’ and ‘not thine’?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This means, “I speak not without the Father, nor say anything of Myself contrary to what seemeth good to Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “These things have I spoken unto you, being yet present with you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since these sayings were not clear, and since some they did not understand, and doubted about the greater number, in order that they might not be again confused, and say, “What commands?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He released them from all their perplexity, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The Comforter, whom the Father shall send in My Name, He shall teach you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Perhaps these things are not clear to you now, but ‘He’ is a clear teacher of them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the, “remaineth with you” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 17 ), is the expression of One implying that Himself will depart.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then that they may not be grieved, He saith, that as long as He should remain with them and the Spirit should not come, they would be unable to comprehend anything great or sublime.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this He said to prepare them to bear nobly His departure, as that which was to be the cause of great blessings to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He continually calleth Him “Comforter,” because of the afflictions which then possessed them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And since even after hearing these things they were troubled, when they thought of the sorrows, the wars, His departure, see how He calmeth them again by saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Peace I leave to you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: All but saying, “What are ye harmed by the trouble of the world, provided ye be at peace with Me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this peace is not of the same kind as that.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The one is external, is often mischievous and unprofitable, and is no advantage to those who possess it; but I give you peace of such a kind that ye be at peace with one another, which thing rendereth you stronger.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And because He said again, “I leave,” which was the expression of One departing, and enough to confound them, therefore He again saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that they were affected partly by loving affection, partly by fear?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye have heard how I said unto you, I go away, and come again unto you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If ye loved Me, ye would rejoice because I said, I go unto the Father; for My Father is greater than I.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what joy would this bring to them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What consolation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then mean the words?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They did not yet know concerning the Resurrection, nor had they right opinion concerning Him; (for how could they, who did not even know that He would rise again?) but they thought that the Father was mighty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He saith then, that “If ye are fearful for Me, as not able to defend Myself, and if ye are not confident that I shall see you again after the Crucifixion, yet when ye heard that I go to the Father, ye ought then to have rejoiced because I go away to One that is greater, and able to undo all dangers.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye have heard how I said unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why hath He put this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because, He saith, “I am so firmly confident about the things which come to pass, that I even foretell them, so far am I from fearing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This also is the meaning of what follows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And now I have told you before it come to pass, that when it is come to pass, ye might believe that I Am.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As though He had said, “Ye would not have known, had I not told you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And I should not have told you, had I not been confident.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that the speech is one of condescension? for when He saith, “Think ye that I cannot pray to the Father, and He shall presently give Me more than twelve legions of Angels” ( Matt. xxvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 53 ), He speaketh to the secret thoughts of the hearers; since no one, even in the height of madness, would say that He was not able to help Himself, but needed Angels; but because they thought of Him as a man, therefore He spoke of “twelve legions of Angels.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet in truth He did but ask those who came to take Him a question, and cast them backwards. ( c. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: (If any one say that the Father is greater, inasmuch as He is the cause of the Son, we will not contradict this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this doth not by any means make the Son to be of a different Essence.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what He saith, is of this kind: “As long as I am here, it is natural that you should deem that I am in danger; but when I am gone ‘there,’ be confident that I am in safety; for Him none will be able to overcome.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: All these words were addressed to the weakness of the disciples, for, “I Myself am confident, and care not for death.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account, He said, “I have told you these things before they come to pass”; “but since,” He saith, “ye are not yet able to receive the saying concerning them, I bring you comfort even from the Father, whom ye entitle great.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Having thus consoled them, He again telleth them sorrowful things,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Hereafter I will not talk with you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For the ruler of this world cometh, and hath nothing in Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By “ruler of this world,” He meaneth the devil, calling wicked men also by the same name.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he ruleth not heaven and earth, since he would have been subverted, and cast down all things, but he ruleth over those who give themselves up to him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He calleth him, “the ruler of the darkness of this world,” in this place again calling evil deeds, “darkness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What then, doth the devil slay Thee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By no means; “he hath nothing in Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How then do they kill Thee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because I will it, and,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 31. “‘That the world may know that I love the Father.’”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For being not subject,” He saith, “to death, nor a debtor to it, I endure it through My love to the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This He saith, that He may again rouse their souls, and that they may learn that not unwillingly but willingly He goeth to this thing, and that He doth it despising the devil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was not enough for Him to have said, “Yet a little while I am with you” ( c. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 33 ), but He continually handleth this painful subject, (with good reason,) until He should make it acceptable to them, by weaving along with it pleasant things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore at one time He saith, “I go, and I come again”; and, “That where I there ye may be also”; and, “Ye cannot follow Me now, but afterwards ye shall follow Me”; and, “I go to the Father”; and, “The Father is greater than I”; and, “Before it come to pass, I have told you”; and, “I do not suffer these things from constraint, but from love for the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that they might consider, that the action could not be destructive nor hurtful, if at least He who greatly loved Him, and was greatly loved by Him, so willed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account, while intermingling these pleasant words, He continually uttered the painful ones also, practicing their minds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For both the, “remaineth with you” ( c. xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 7 ), and, “My departure is expedient for you,” were expressions of One giving comfort.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this reason He spake by anticipation ten thousand sayings concerning the Spirit, the, “Is in you,” and, “The world cannot receive,” and, “He shall bring all things to your remembrance,” and, “Spirit of truth,” and, “Holy Spirit,” and, “Comforter,” and that “It is expedient for you,” in order that they might not despond, as though there would be none to stand before and help them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “It is expedient,” He saith, showing that It would make them spiritual.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This at least, we see, was what took place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they who now trembled and feared, after they had received the Spirit sprang into the midst of dangers, and stripped themselves for the contest against steel, and fire, and wild beasts, and seas, and every kind of punishment; and they, the unlettered and ignorant, discoursed so boldly as to astonish their hearers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the Spirit made them men of iron instead of men of clay, gave them wings, and allowed them to be cast down by nothing human.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For such is that grace; if it find despondency, it disperses it; if evil desires, it consumes them; if cowardice, it casts it out, and doth not allow one who has partaken of it to be afterwards mere man, but as it were removing him to heaven itself, causes him to image to himself all that is there. ( Acts iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account no one said that any of the things that he possessed was his own, but they continued in prayer, in praise, and in singleness of heart.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this the Holy Spirit most requireth, for “the fruit of the Spirit is joy, peace—faith, meek ness.” ( Gal. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And yet spiritual persons often grieve,” saith some one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But that sorrow is sweeter than joy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Cain was sorrowful, but with the sorrow of the world; Paul was sorrowful, but with godly sorrow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Everything that is spiritual brings the greatest gain, just as everything that is worldly the utmost loss.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then draw to us the invincible aid of the Spirit, by keeping the commandments, and then we shall be nothing inferior to the Angels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For neither are they therefore of this character, because they are incorporeal, for were this the case, no incorporeal being would have become wicked, but the will is in every case the cause of all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore among incorporeal beings some have been found worse than men or things irrational, and among those having bodies some better than the incorporeal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: All just men, for instance, whatever were their righteous deeds, did them while dwelling on earth, and having bodies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they dwelt on earth as those who were pilgrims and strangers; but in heaven, as citizens.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then say not thou either, “I am clothed with flesh, I cannot get the mastery, nor undertake the toils which are for the sake of virtue.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do not accuse the Creator.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if the wearing the flesh make virtue impossible, then the fault is not ours.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But that it does not make it impossible, the band of saints has shown.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A nature of flesh did not prevent Paul from becoming what he was, nor Peter from receiving the keys of heaven; and Enoch also, having worn flesh, was translated, and not found.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So also Elias was caught up with the flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Abraham also with Isaac and his grandson shone brightly, having the flesh; and Joseph in the flesh struggled against that abandoned woman.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why speak I of the flesh?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For though thou place a chain upon the flesh, no harm is done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Though I am bound,” saith Paul, yet “the word of God is not bound.” ( 2 Tim. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And why speak I of bonds and chains?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Add to these the prison, and bars, yet neither are these any hindrance to virtue; at least so Paul hath instructed us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the bond of the soul is not iron but cowardice, and the desire of wealth, and the ten thousand passions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These bind us, though our body be free.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” saith some one, “these have their origin from the body.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: An excuse this, and a false pretense.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had they been produced from the body, all would have undergone them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as we cannot escape weariness, and sleep, and hunger, and thirst, since they belong to our nature; so too these, if they were of the same kind, would not allow any one to be exempt from their tyranny; but since many escape them, it is clear that such things are the faults of a careless soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then put a stop to this, and not accuse the body, but subdue it to the soul, that having it under command, we may enjoy the everlasting good things, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory for ever and ever.
